revision_controls.conditional_claim: eta_eff is a first-order predictor when boundary fragility and backend semantic mismatch are bounded.

steps: Generate a matched threshold grid over code family, distance, eta, p, schedule, decoder, and seed.
Estimate nominal biased-Pauli components and compiled eta_eff values from schedule-aware gate budgets.
Fit nominal-eta and eta_eff threshold predictors and compare holdout errors.
Write the threshold-grid dataset, bias summary tables, and collapse figures.
success_criteria: Eta_eff-based collapse reduces holdout RMSE by at least 20% relative to nominal eta for standard_surface and xzzx.
Collapse dispersion is lower under eta_eff in at least three of four bias settings.
The ideal-curve residuals track |eta_eff-eta| more strongly than eta alone.

steps: Generate gate-family ablations over five gate classes, three ablation modes, four distances, four eta values, and five seeds.
Compute alpha_g, r_g, and chi_temp alpha_g r_g contributions.
Audit interval-bound compliance and benchmark predictors of delta eta_eff.
success_criteria: The convex-mixture interval bound has zero empirical violations.
The top-2 gate classes explain at least 70% of the absolute eta_eff shift in each ablation family.
The convex-mixture predictor improves delta-eta_eff MAE by at least 25% relative to the weakest baselines.

steps: Generate one-factor perturbations in temporal order, reset asymmetry, CNOT decomposition, and idle padding.
Compare predicted and observed eta_eff directional derivatives.
Evaluate the transfer bound K|eta_eff-eta|+epsilon_model on held-out schedules.
success_criteria: Derivative sign accuracy is at least 80%.
Replacing nominal eta with eta_eff lowers holdout RMSE by at least 20%.
The empirical transfer residual stays within the fitted bound for at least 90% of held-out points.

steps: Generate finite-distance logical-rate grids and bootstrap resamples.
Compare naive pairwise crossings to a hierarchical finite-size attribution model.
Write threshold, penalty, and model comparison tables plus supporting figures.
success_criteria: The hierarchical model improves held-out negative log-likelihood by at least 15%.
Removing decoder or schedule penalties worsens WAIC by at least 5 points.
The bootstrap 95% interval width for p_th_infinity stays below 0.01 in at least three of four bias settings.

steps: Generate matched Aer and Stim surrogate outcomes with first- and second-order detector translations.
Benchmark parity, detector discrepancy, threshold gap, and runtime speedup.
Write parity figures and backend agreement tables.
success_criteria: The second-order translation yields median logical-error relative error at or below 10% for low- and medium-fragility schedules.
The threshold absolute gap stays below 0.2 percentage points on at least 75% of matched configurations.
The second-order translation improves detector-pair KL divergence by at least 30% relative to first order.

steps: Generate matched boundary variants for standard_surface and xzzx across bias, distance, and schedule conditions.
Fit the interaction model linking code family, log eta, and boundary fragility.
Write interaction figures and coefficient tables with uncertainty.
success_criteria: The interaction term beta_5 is positive with a 95% confidence interval that excludes zero in at least one high-bias regime.
The interaction model lowers held-out RMSE by at least 15% relative to the fragility-agnostic baseline.
The finite-distance XZZX advantage retention drops monotonically with boundary fragility in at least two bias settings.
